fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

seo.title: Michigan Dietitian Nutritionist License Reciprocity - How to Transfer Your License (2026)
seo.description: Already licensed as a dietitian nutritionist? Here's whether your license transfers to Michigan - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.
seo.keywords: dietitian nutritionist license reciprocity michigan, how to transfer dietitian nutritionist license to michigan, dietitian nutritionist compact michigan, michigan dietitian nutritionist endorsement application

board.name: Board of Dietetics and Nutrition
board.parent_dept: Michigan Department of Licensing and Regulatory Affairs (LARA)
board.mailing_address: Michigan Department of Licensing and Regulatory Affairs, Bureau of Professional Licensing, P.O. Box 30670, Lansing, MI 48909-8170

quick_facts.fee_breakdown: $75 application processing fee + $55 license fee (per year)

license_types.title: Licensed Dietitian Nutritionist (LDN)
license_types.description: Michigan reinstated state licensure for dietitian nutritionists through Public Act 39 of 2024, signed April 30, 2024. The Board of Dietetics and Nutrition was appointed and approved administrative rules in October 2025, but the application process is expected to open 18 months after rules are promulgated.

license_types.title: Temporary Licensed Dietitian Nutritionist
license_types.description: A temporary license will be available for $55 once the application process opens.

steps.title: Create a MiPLUS Account
steps.description: Visit the Michigan Professional Licensing User System (MiPLUS) portal at aca-prod.accela.com/LARA/Default.aspx and click 'Register for an Account' to establish your user profile.

steps.title: Initiate Online Application
steps.description: Log in to your newly created MiPLUS account, select 'Apply for a License,' and choose 'Dietitian' from the list of available license types to begin the application process.

steps.title: Complete Application and Upload Documents
steps.description: Fill out all required sections of the online application within MiPLUS. This includes providing personal details, educational history, proof of supervised experience, and verification of your Commission on Dietetic Registration (CDR) certification. Ensure official transcripts are sent directly from your institution if required.

steps.title: Pay Application Fee
steps.description: Submit the non-refundable application fee of $75 as prompted within the MiPLUS system at the time of application submission.

steps.title: Monitor Application Status
steps.description: Regularly log in to your MiPLUS account to track the status of your application. Respond promptly to any requests for additional information or documentation from the Bureau of Professional Licensing (BPL) to avoid delays.

documents.detail: Proof of active registration with the Commission on Dietetic Registration (RD/RDN).
Application form will be available through the LARA online licensing portal.

provisional_text: Michigan's new Board of Dietetics and Nutrition will offer a temporary license for $55 once the application process is fully operational.
reciprocity_text: Reciprocity provisions will be defined by the newly formed Board. Active CDR registration will be the primary qualifying credential, making the transition straightforward for out-of-state licensees.

faqs.question: Is Michigan currently accepting dietitian license applications?
faqs.answer: Not yet. Although Public Act 39 of 2024 reinstated licensure and the Board approved administrative rules in October 2025, applications are expected to open approximately 18 months after the rules are formally promulgated.

faqs.question: How much will a Michigan dietitian license cost?
faqs.answer: The established fees are a $75 application processing fee and a $55 annual license fee, for a total initial cost of $130.

faqs.question: Can I practice in Michigan without a state license right now?
faqs.answer: Yes. Until the application process opens and the law is fully enforced, practicing with a CDR credential in Michigan remains the standard. Most employers require the RD/RDN credential.

faqs.question: Does Michigan have reciprocity for dietitian nutritionists?
faqs.answer: Michigan is currently a CDR State. There is no separate state endorsement transfer pathway right now; maintain an active CDR credential to practice.

faqs.question: How long does it take to transfer my dietitian nutritionist license to Michigan?
faqs.answer: This is currently a CDR State, so there is no state transfer timeline. You can usually start once your CDR credential and employer onboarding are complete.

faqs.question: Can I start working in Michigan while my endorsement application is processing?
faqs.answer: There is no active state endorsement application to wait on right now. Start dates are typically driven by employer credentialing and onboarding requirements.

reciprocity.notes: Reciprocity provisions will be defined by the newly formed Board. Active CDR registration will be the primary qualifying credential, making the transition straightforward for out-of-state licensees.
